# Phylo view

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="49%"}
::: {style="font-size:1rem;"}

Pros:

- mathematically low-hanging fruit to consider putative transmission pairs
- can handle $> 100,000$ taxa
- easy to adjust for differential sampling
$$\begin{align*}
  z_{ab} &\sim \text{Poisson}\big( \lambda_{ab} \big)\\
  n_{ab} &\sim \text{Binom}\big( z_{ab}, \zeta_a\zeta_b \big)\\
  \Rightarrow&\\
  n_{ab} &\sim \text{Poisson}\big( \lambda_{ab} \zeta_a\zeta_b \big)
\end{align*}
$$
- standard regression and regularisation tools available
$$
\log \lambda_{ab} = \lambda_0 + \lambda^T_1 X_a + \lambda^T_2 X_b + \lambda^T_3 X_{ab}
$$
- standard inference tools available, Stan, numpyro


:::
:::
::: {.column width="49%"}
::: {style="font-size:1rem;"}

Cons:

- Discard a lot of data
- Heuristic construction

:::
:::
:::
